title: cryptocarya coriacea
intro: Cryptocarya coriacea, also known as the "sassafras tree," is a species of tree in the laurel family (Lauraceae) that is native to the tropical forests of Australia and Southeast Asia. The bark and roots of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cryptocarya coriacea include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Cryptocarya coriacea can be made by drying the bark or roots and infusing them in hot water to create a tea, or by using them in tincture or capsule form.
